title: Is the quality of our inland waters good enough for swimming?
subtitle: In Austria, Finland, Denmark, Luxembourg and Germany, at least 90 per cent of inland bathing waters – typically lakes – were classified as 'excellent', but in Slovenia, Portugal, Poland, Croatia and Spain, the figure was less than 60 per cent.
textNote: Some countries may have lower scores for ‘excellent’ quality, whilst still having ‘good’ quality waters.
